message:
fix(h1): preserve handler Content-Length in HEAD response (RFC 9110 §9.3.2)

suppress_response_body_for_head() unconditionally replaced the handler's
Content-Length with the actual body length. RFC 9110 §9.3.2 requires HEAD
responses to carry the same Content-Length as the equivalent GET response.
When the handler explicitly declares Content-Length, trust it as the
authoritative GET length instead of overwriting.

Updated test to validate RFC-compliant behavior.

Also audited h1/server.rs (1488L): request smuggling protections, 100-continue,
keep-alive, shutdown integration — all SOUND.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -581,3 +581,11 @@
 "SOUND"
 0
 "Cancel drains instead of drops in Polling state, attempt counting correct, clean state machine"
+"src/http/h1/server.rs"
+1488
+408
+"2026-03-21"
+"SapphireHill"
+"FIXED"
+1
+"1 LOW: HEAD response overwrites handler Content-Length, violating RFC 9110 §9.3.2"
